title: Sentinel Protocol v3.1 – Infrastructure Pre-Public Deployment Audit Log
author: Telles, Fernando
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Sentinel Protocol v3.1 – Infrastructure Reproducibility Audit Log ("Ordinal 05") 
 Validator-signed, cryptographically anchored audit for SENTINFRA-SESS001. 
 Enforces zero-custody reproducibility via Bitcoin timestamps and hashes — no trust in CDA AI or Sentinel required. 
 
 📎 RIPEMD160(SHA256 of file): 
 f6fb3b90e2721ea7554bb0c7ed65aa24c466d414 
 🔗 Ordinal 06 (Full Verification Guide): 
 e11022c0ae5b6d603a815e17eeb3af2573cf3cf0ca4f5372da7b63b04dc7ebe3 
 ⬇️ PDF: `Ordinal_05_..._FINAL_METADATA___20250729T212151.538870Z.pdf` 
 
 --- 
 
 🔍 **How to Verify (Zero Trust – 3 Steps):** 
 1. Download exact PDF (unaltered) 
 2. Run: `openssl sha256 file.pdf` → then `echo -n "<SHA256>" | xxd -r -p | openssl ripemd160` 
 3. Match result to hash above. Confirm TXID 
 2fc3200bde4757a679336d64058cc72d163f87f3b7d9afcea8e632984cc4077b 
 on mempool.space for Bitcoin-anchored timestamp. 
 
 This confirms the PDF is unaltered, canonical, and timestamp-anchored on Bitcoin. 
 
 ✅ Sentinel Protocol enables reproducibility without exposure — for medicine, science, law, and cryptographic public audit. 
 See Ordinal 06 for full guide. 
 
 ⚠️ Note: Some platforms (e.g., ResearchGate) may append metadata pages to uploaded PDFs. To verify the official audit hash, download the file from: 
 
 🔗 https://github.com/TELAISYN 
 🔗 https://aihumansynergy.org
